ENGINEER
Available for work · Melbourne, VIC

MITALI
KAUR

From India's Railways to AWS — Electronics Engineer turned Cloud Technologist. 4+ years delivering safety-critical systems for Indian Railways — HVAC, VR simulators, braking systems, fire safety. Now building cloud infrastructure on AWS. Master of Data Science (Cloud Computing), Deakin University.

View Projects Get in Touch
scroll
01 — About

I'm an Electronics & Telecommunications Engineer with over four years of hands-on experience delivering complex, safety-critical systems for India's rail industry — working alongside global OEMs including Siemens, Alstom, Knorr-Bremse, BEML, BHEL, REDS SA Poland, Parker Hannifin, Faiveley Transport, Wabtec, Bluestar, and Clark Filter USA.

My engineering career spans the full product lifecycle: from new product development and simulation through to on-site commissioning, quality audits, and stakeholder sign-off. I've delivered HVAC systems for the Vande Bharat Express, India's first VR driver training simulator, vacuum toilet systems, fire and smoke detection, axle-mounted disc braking, and LHB electrical panels — all certified to IRIS (ISO/TS 22163) and ISO 9001 standards for Indian Railways customers including ICF Chennai, Siemens, and Alstom.

At Tayal & Co. — one of India's leading IRIS-certified suppliers of safety-critical rail components for 25+ years — I led quality engineering across braking systems, air suspension, FIBA devices, smoke detection, and inter-coach gangways, coordinating with international OEMs including Knorr-Bremse (Germany), Faiveley Transport (France), and Wabtec (USA) and maintaining full RDSO compliance. At GVM Rail / Amit Engineers — India's largest rail HVAC manufacturer, supplying Siemens, Alstom, BEML, BHEL, Medha, RITES, CGL, Titagarh, and Indian Railways — I managed new product development across HVAC, vacuum systems, fire detection, and electrical panels, reducing operational overhead by 60% through ERP automation.

Now completing a Master of Data Science (Cloud Computing) at Deakin University — with completed units in AWS Cloud Computing, Cyber Security Management, Machine Learning, Deep Learning, Data Wrangling, Statistics, and a research thesis on the Bi-DBRSCAN clustering model — I'm applying the same engineering rigour to cloud infrastructure, CI/CD automation, and data science.

I bring the precision of safety-critical engineering to everything I build — whether it's a rail HVAC system certified to IRIS standards or a cloud architecture on AWS. I'm detail-driven, calm under pressure, and thrive in environments where reliability matters.

Based in Melbourne, VIC. Full work rights. Actively seeking cloud, data engineering, and engineering operations roles.

03 — Projects
Selected Work
★ Flagship Project

Rail · VR Technology 2018–2019
ECOSAFE VR — Train-18 Virtual Reality Driver Simulator
Prag Polymers · ICF Chennai · REDS SA Poland

India's first Virtual Reality driver training simulator, purpose-built for the Vande Bharat Express (Train-18). Features a full-scale replica driver cab with all original switches and controls, immersive VR scenarios, energy-efficiency scoring, and emergency response simulation under adverse weather. Personally signed the official ICF commissioning certificate.

India's first VR train simulator — national landmark project
Signed official ICF commissioning certificate as Engineer, E&E
Led 40+ person cross-functional team across 3 training cycles
International collaboration: REDS SA, Warsaw
VR Systems Hardware Integration Electrical & Electronics ICF Chennai REDS SA Poland ISO Commissioning

Cloud · AWS 2025
AWS VPC Peering & Multi-Region Network Architecture
github.com/Mita22-1

Designed and deployed multi-VPC AWS network architecture with peering configuration — implementing least-privilege IAM policies, CIDR planning, and secure cross-account routing. Documented architecture decisions as structured runbooks.

AWS VPC IAM Network Architecture CloudFormation Security
Cloud · AWS 2025
Highly Available WordPress Architecture on AWS
github.com/Mita22-1 · Deakin SIT706

Architected a fault-tolerant, highly-available platform on AWS using Elastic Load Balancing, Auto Scaling Groups, and Multi-AZ RDS MySQL. Applied CloudFormation IaC across two stacks — network layer and application layer. Graded HD.

ELB Auto Scaling RDS Multi-AZ CloudFormation IaC
DevOps · CI/CD 2025
GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline Automation
github.com/Mita22-1

Designed and implemented automated build, test, and deploy pipelines using GitHub Actions — eliminating manual deployment steps, applying GitOps principles, and integrating automated testing to catch failures early.

GitHub Actions CI/CD GitOps Automation Testing
Research · ML 2025–2026
↗ Click to view architecture diagram & full details
Bi-DBRSCAN — Bidirectional Clustering Model for Time-Series Data
Deakin University · Supervised by Dr. Musa Mammadov

Developed an intelligent energy-aware resource orchestration framework for cloud-native LLM pipelines — predicting VM workload behaviour before orchestration using novel Bi-DBRSCAN clustering. Achieved 96.12% resource utilisation, 98.86% classification accuracy, and lowest energy consumption vs all baselines. Datasets: NAD (148,517 records) + Bitbrains (1,750 VMs). Target: Springer Nature.

Python Bi-DBRSCAN JPR-AE GGSRU MADRL Cloud-Native LLM Pipelines Springer Nature

Vacuum Systems ICF Specs New Product Dev ISO 9001
Rail · Fire Safety 2022–2023
Fire & Smoke Detection System (FSDS) for Rolling Stock
GVM Rail / Amit Engineers

Worked on safety-critical Fire and Smoke Detection Systems for rail coaches — detecting electrical short circuits, flammable materials, and collision-related fire risks onboard Indian Railways rolling stock, to EN fire protection standards.

Fire Safety FSDS Safety-Critical Rolling Stock

GVM Rail / Amit Engineers

Rail · Safety Systems 2023–2024
Braking, Air Suspension & FIBA Safety Systems
Tayal & Co. (IRIS Certified)

Quality engineering across Tayal's safety-critical product range — Axle Mounted Disc Brake Systems for high-speed trains, Anti-Vibration/Air Suspension systems, Air Suspension Control Equipment, and FIBA (Failure Indication Cum Brake Application) devices for FIAT Bogie coaches.

Disc Brakes Air Suspension FIBA IRIS Certified ISO/TS 22163
Embedded Systems · IoT · Agriculture 2018
Smart Greenhouse Regulation & Protection System
Gautam Buddha University · B.Tech + M.Tech Final Year Project

Designed, simulated, and fabricated an automated smart greenhouse control system using an ATMEGA328P microcontroller. Monitors temperature, soil moisture, water level, humidity, and flame conditions in real time — automatically activating irrigation pumps, sprinklers, ventilation motors, LCD alerts, buzzer alarms, and GSM SMS notifications when environmental thresholds are breached. Full circuit simulated in Proteus across 6 test scenarios before physical fabrication on matrix board.

6 test scenarios validated: normal, low water, low moisture, flame, high temperature, and initialisation
Multi-channel alerts: LCD + buzzer + LED + GSM SMS — all triggered correctly in simulation and hardware
Full hardware prototype fabricated and tested on matrix board — matched simulation results
ATMEGA328P Proteus GSM Module LM35 Sensor Soil Moisture Ultrasonic Flame Sensor Embedded C IoT Automation
Electronics · Power Systems 2016
Feedback-Controlled SMPS Power Regulation System
Gautam Buddha University · B.Tech Minor Project (EC493)

Designed and simulated a multi-stage switched-mode power supply (SMPS) using MOSFET switching, PWM control, and closed-loop feedback mechanisms in Multisim. Built three circuit models covering AC-DC rectification, MOSFET switching, and full regulated output — achieving stable 5V output with negligible ripple after a 2ms startup. Validated via oscilloscope waveform analysis across all subsystems.

Stable 5V regulated output with <2ms startup and negligible ripple
PWM-sine synchronisation confirmed across all feedback stages
Validated galvanic isolation maintaining signal integrity
SMPS PWM MOSFET Multisim Feedback Control LM7805CT ICS 31.080
Embedded Systems · IoT 2017
Vehicle Status Monitoring & Alert Mechanism System
Gautam Buddha University · B.Tech Major Project (EC592)

Designed and simulated a real-time vehicle monitoring and emergency alert system using ATMEGA1284P microcontroller, GPS, GSM, accelerometer, and vibration sensors in Proteus. System detects vibration and tilt events, activates LED/buzzer alerts, displays status on LCD, and transmits GPS coordinates via GSM SMS — validated across initialization, normal, vibration, and tilt scenarios with consistent response times.

Detected vibration and tilt events with real-time LCD status updates
GSM SMS alerts with live GPS coordinates transmitted on threshold breach
Resolved baud rate mismatch (set to 9600 bps) restoring full GSM communication
ATMEGA1284P GSM Module GPS Tracking Proteus Accelerometer Embedded C UART/USART ICS 33.070.50
Cybersecurity · Research 2024–2025
Network Security Simulation — Blackhole Attack (NS-3)
Deakin University · SIT716

Simulated and analysed blackhole attack scenarios in network environments using NS-3. Modelled vulnerability patterns, measured attack impact on routing, and proposed mitigation strategies — supporting ACSC Essential Eight security awareness.

NS-3 Network Security Cybersecurity Threat Analysis ACSC E8

Master's Research Paper · Deakin University · 2025–2026
Intelligent & Energy-Aware Resource Orchestration via Workload Behaviour Prediction using JPR-AE and MADRL
Supervised by Dr. Musa Mammadov · Implemented in Python · Target Publication: Springer Nature (Q1)

Developed a complete end-to-end intelligent resource orchestration framework for cloud-native LLM pipelines. The system predicts VM workload behaviour before orchestration — detecting anomalies, grouping workloads, analysing trends, and efficiently allocating virtual resources — all while minimising energy consumption. Trained and evaluated on the Network Anomaly Detection (NAD) dataset (148,517 records) and Grid Workloads Archive-T-12 Bitbrains dataset (1,750 VMs, ~5,000 data points each).

Key Results
96.1%
Resource Utilisation (1000 workloads)
98.9%
GGSRU Classification Accuracy
98.5%
JPR-AE Detection Accuracy
0.963
Bi-DBRSCAN Silhouette Score
0.125
ARIRIMA RMSE (vs 0.325 ARIMA)
13,547 mJ
Energy Consumption (lowest vs all baselines)
Pipeline Components
ANOMALY DETECTION
GGSRU
Gish Gated Switchable Recurrent Unit — 98.86% accuracy, 98.75% precision, 98.65% recall, 98.41% F-measure
WORKLOAD BEHAVIOUR
JPR-AE
Jacobian Posterior Regularised AutoEncoder — 98.52% detection accuracy, reconstruction error 0.41 (vs AE: 1.75)
WORKLOAD GROUPING
Bi-DBRSCAN
Novel algorithm — Silhouette Score 0.963, Grouping Time 13,254ms (vs DBSCAN: 18,693ms, K-Means: 28,743ms)
TREND ANALYSIS
ARIRIMA
AutoRegressive Integrated Rolling Interquartile Moving Average — MSE: 0.352, RMSE: 0.125, MAE: 0.236
CORRELATION ANALYSIS
ICC
Intra-Class Correlation — analyses hidden constraints between VM feature resource consumptions
RESOURCE ORCHESTRATION
MADRL
Multi-Agent Dense Reward Learning — 96.12% utilisation, 6,589ms latency, 12,457ms throughput for 1000 workloads
Datasets Used
ANOMALY DETECTION TRAINING
Network Anomaly Detection (NAD)
148,517 records · 69,975 normal + 78,542 attack · 80/20 train-test split
WORKLOAD BEHAVIOUR TRAINING
Grid Workloads Archive-T-12 Bitbrains
1,750 VMs · ~5,000 data points/VM · distributed data centre · 80/20 split
Proposed Bi-DBRSCAN outperformed DBSCAN, PAM, K-Means, and FCM — achieving fastest grouping time (13,254ms) and highest silhouette score (0.963)
JPR-AE achieved 98.52% detection accuracy with minimum reconstruction error of 0.41 — outperforming AE (96.33%), AAE (94.83%), DAE (92.23%), VAE (90.87%)
MADRL achieved 96.12% resource utilisation for 1,000 workloads — response time 10,236ms vs DQN's 18,472ms, energy consumption 13,547mJ vs DQN's highest
GGSRU classification: 98.86% accuracy, 98.75% precision — outperforming GRU, BiLSTM, LSTM, and RNN across all metrics
Addresses key research gap: traditional orchestration methods ignored workload behaviour variation before orchestration, leading to energy waste and security vulnerabilities
Bi-DBRSCAN JPR-AE GGSRU ARIRIMA MADRL ICC Python Cloud-Native LLM Pipelines Energy-Aware Anomaly Detection Springer Nature

TREND ANALYSIS
ARIRIMA
AutoRegressive Integrated Rolling Interquartile Moving Average — MSE: 0.352, RMSE: 0.125, MAE: 0.236
CORRELATION ANALYSIS
ICC
Intra-Class Correlation — analyses hidden constraints between VM feature resource consumptions
RESOURCE ORCHESTRATION
MADRL
Multi-Agent Dense Reward Learning — 96.12% utilisation, 6,589ms latency, 12,457ms throughput for 1000 workloads
Datasets Used
ANOMALY DETECTION TRAINING
Network Anomaly Detection (NAD)
148,517 records · 69,975 normal + 78,542 attack · 80/20 train-test split
WORKLOAD BEHAVIOUR TRAINING
Grid Workloads Archive-T-12 Bitbrains
1,750 VMs · ~5,000 data points/VM · distributed data centre · 80/20 split
Proposed Bi-DBRSCAN outperformed DBSCAN, PAM, K-Means, and FCM — achieving fastest grouping time (13,254ms) and highest silhouette score (0.963)
JPR-AE achieved 98.52% detection accuracy with minimum reconstruction error of 0.41 — outperforming AE (96.33%), AAE (94.83%), DAE (92.23%), VAE (90.87%)
MADRL achieved 96.12% resource utilisation for 1,000 workloads — response time 10,236ms vs DQN's 18,472ms, energy consumption 13,547mJ vs DQN's highest
GGSRU classification: 98.86% accuracy, 98.75% precision — outperforming GRU, BiLSTM, LSTM, and RNN across all metrics
Addresses key research gap: traditional orchestration methods ignored workload behaviour variation before orchestration, leading to energy waste and security vulnerabilities
Bi-DBRSCAN JPR-AE GGSRU ARIRIMA MADRL ICC Python Cloud-Native LLM Pipelines Energy-Aware Anomaly Detection Springer Nature

← Left to right: Witold Bartnik (REDS SA, Warsaw) · Mitali Kaur (Prag Polymers) · N. Udaya Kumar (Deputy Chief ME, ICF Chennai) — holding the signed commissioning certificate, 4 June 2019

★ Flagship Project
ECOSAFE VR — Train-18 Virtual Reality Driver Simulator
Prag Polymers · ICF Chennai · REDS SA Warsaw · 2018–2019

India's first Virtual Reality driver training simulator, purpose-built for the Vande Bharat Express (Train-18). Features a full-scale replica driver cab with all original switches and controls, immersive VR scenarios using Oculus Rift, energy-efficiency scoring, and emergency response simulation under adverse weather conditions. Inaugurated on 18th December 2018 by Smt. D. Renukadevi, Record Supplier, in the presence of ICF's team of officers and staff.

Client
ICF / Indian Railways
Location
ICF Chennai, India
Technology Partner
REDS SA, Warsaw
Spec
ICF/MDD/SPEC-341
Inauguration
18 Dec 2018
Commissioned
04 Jun 2019
Training Sessions
Jan, Mar, Jun 2019
VR Platform
Oculus Rift · 92fps · 12ms
Route
35.97km virtual route
Usability Score
4.7 / 5.0
India's first VR train simulator — national landmark project for Indian Railways
Personally signed official ICF commissioning certificate as Engineer — Electrical & Electronics, alongside N. Udaya Kumar (Deputy Chief ME/DSS, ICF) and Witold Bartnik (Product Manager, REDS SA)
Led 40+ person cross-functional team across all 3 training cycles at ICF Design & Development Centre
System achieved 12ms head-tracking latency, 92fps rendering, 4.7/5 usability score after iterative improvements
Onboarded 22 loco pilots across training cycles — simulator later recommended for nationwide rollout
